_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**Research gap:** No announced SmartWay rulemaking, program restructuring, or scheduled EPA guidance publication with a confirmed date has been identified from the source material available for this brief. The EPA SmartWay page was last updated April 14, 2026, and contains no notice of pending structural changes to the program.

The following adjacent regulatory events are confirmed and may affect SmartWay's role in the workspace's reporting obligations:

- **EPA Heavy-Duty Vehicle GHG Phase 3 Rule (89 Fed. Reg. 29440, April 22, 2024):** This rule establishes GHG emissions standards for new heavy-duty vehicles in model years 2027–2032. As the carrier fleet subject to these standards renews, SmartWay's carrier performance rankings — which measure fleet efficiency — will reflect the updated baseline. Workspaces using SmartWay carrier data for Scope 3 calculations should expect the benchmark distribution to shift as newer-standard vehicles enter the fleet. *Source: Federal Register, EPA, April 22, 2024. https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2024/04/22/2024-06809/greenhouse-gas-emissions-standards-for-heavy-duty-vehicles-phase-3*

- **U.S. DOT National Freight Strategic Plan 2025 Update (Request for Information, July 15, 2025):** DOT issued an RFI for the 2025 NFSP update, which may address freight emissions benchmarking frameworks and the role of voluntary programs like SmartWay in national freight strategy. Outcome not yet published as of this brief. *Source: Federal Register, U.S. Department of Transportation, July 15, 2025. https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2025/07/15/2025-13219/national-freight-strategic-plan-2025-update-request-for-information*
